task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the grading interface for the 'Lab 3: Titration Analysis' assignment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the Moodle page for 'Lab 3: Titration Analysis', such as the assignment summary, 'View all submissions' page, or an individual student's grading page.

task_completion.description: Jane Smith is assigned a grade of 85.00 with the correct feedback.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Moodle grading interface (either the submissions table or individual grading page), Jane Smith's grade is set to 85.00 and the feedback text includes 'error margins' and 'section 2'.

task_completion.description: Michael Jones is assigned a grade of 92.50 with the correct feedback.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Moodle grading interface, Michael Jones's grade is set to 92.50 and the feedback text includes 'Excellent graphs' and 'Perfect result'.

task_completion.description: Alice Wilson is assigned a grade of 78.00 with the correct feedback.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Moodle grading interface, Alice Wilson's grade is set to 78.00 and the feedback text includes 'Missing', 'conclusion', and 'rubric'.

task_completion.description: The grades and feedback are saved in the Moodle system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A confirmation message such as 'Changes saved' is visible, or the 'View all submissions' table clearly shows the updated grades and feedback as saved text (or within saved quick-grading fields after a save action).

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser to interact with Moodle.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Moodle interface is visible in a web browser like Chromium or Firefox, not a terminal or script.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine Moodle application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the standard Moodle UI elements (navigation bar, grading tables, etc.) and not a mock-up or text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing SQL commands, curl requests, or scripts used to update the Moodle database directly.
